scripts.prebuild: node scripts/web.mjs svelte-kit sync && npm run gen:icons && npm run gen:releases
scripts.prebuild:cap: node scripts/web.mjs svelte-kit sync && npm run gen:icons && npm run gen:releases
scripts.build:cap: CAPACITOR=true node scripts/web.mjs vite build && node --experimental-strip-types --disable-warning=ExperimentalWarning scripts/strip-native-assets.mjs
scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:asset-gen && npm run test:e2e
scripts.cap:android: npm run cap:sync && cap open android
scripts.cap:ios: npm run cap:sync && cap open ios
scripts.android:emulator: npm run cap:sync && cap run android --target Pixel_7_Pro_API_33
scripts.android:run:device: npm run cap:sync && ANDROID_SERIAL=R5CY128YMGF node scripts/gradle.mjs :app:installDebug
scripts.ios:build: npm run cap:sync && cd ios/App && xcodebuild -scheme App -configuration Debug -destination "generic/platform=iOS Simulator" -derivedDataPath build build
scripts.ios:run: npm run cap:sync && cap run ios
scripts.ios:run:emulator: npm run cap:sync && cap run ios --target C6012C49-AA93-4869-B3A6-E47C9EAAC567
scripts.ios:run:device: npm run cap:sync && cap run ios --target 00008103-0006202E3CF1001E
scripts.ios:archive: npm run cap:sync && cd ios/App && xcodebuild -scheme App -configuration Release -destination generic/platform=iOS -archivePath build/App.xcarchive -xcconfig ../local.xcconfig -allowProvisioningUpdates archive
scripts.ios:ipa: npm run ios:archive && cd ios/App && xcodebuild -exportArchive -archivePath build/App.xcarchive -exportOptionsPlist ExportOptions.plist -exportPath build/ipa -allowProvisioningUpdates
scripts.prerelease: npm run check:assets && npm run check:assets:manifest

scripts-info.info: Lists every npm script with its description (this output)
scripts-info.predev:netlify: Checks the Netlify CLI is on PATH, logged in, and linked before dev:netlify (Blobs needs auth), with a hint if not
scripts-info.dev:netlify: Dev server with the /api/* serverless functions (Netlify Dev)
scripts-info.dev:host: Dev server exposed on the local network (for phones and other devices)
scripts-info.dev:cap: Dev server in native mode (CAPACITOR=true) on the network, for android:live
scripts-info.dev:tunnel: Claude Code Cloud only: start dev + a chisel reverse tunnel and print the public phone-preview URL (needs TUNNEL_AUTH; see docs/CLOUD/Claude.md, ADR-0021)
scripts-info.dev:kill: Kill any stale dev servers holding the dev ports (vite 5173, netlify dev 8888)
scripts-info.prebuild: svelte-kit sync + gen:icons + gen:releases before build
scripts-info.prebuild:cap: svelte-kit sync + gen:icons + gen:releases before build:cap
scripts-info.build:cap: Native static build (CAPACITOR=true, adapter-static); strips web-only coloring-book assets
scripts-info.preview: Serve the production web build locally
scripts-info.precheck: Generate .svelte-kit types (svelte-kit sync) so type checking can run standalone
scripts-info.check:assets: Validate that every coloring-book asset exists on disk (auto-runs before release)
scripts-info.check:assets:manifest: CI guard for gen:assets:manifest: fail if any committed art asset's bytes drifted from tools/asset-gen/golden/asset-manifest.sha256 (e.g. a night pass touched a light file); run gen:assets:manifest and commit to fix
scripts-info.img:audit: Optimize every shipped/inlined SVG under web/ in place with SVGO (visually-lossless preset; keeps viewBox). Idempotent — re-running only rewrites new/edited SVGs. Skips generator-input SVGs (large-image.svg, styles/source.svg)
scripts-info.img:audit:check: CI guard for img:audit: fail if any SVG under web/ isn't already optimized (drift detector; run img:audit and commit to fix)
scripts-info.lint: ESLint over the whole repo minus the ignores in eslint.config.js (flat config; enforces runes-only, no legacy stores)
scripts-info.format: Format the whole repo: Prettier for source, dprint for markdown (json/yml still excluded)
scripts-info.format:check: Check Prettier + dprint formatting without writing — what CI runs
scripts-info.format:md: Format all markdown docs with dprint (asterisk bullets/emphasis, wrap at 100, ADR-0057)
scripts-info.format:md:check: Check markdown formatting without writing — part of format:check
scripts-info.ruler:apply: Regenerate every agent file (root + nested CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/) from the .ruler/ sources, then dprint-format the output (ADR-0058)
scripts-info.ruler:check: CI drift guard for ruler:apply: re-apply and fail if any generated agent file changed — fix by running ruler:apply and committing (never edit generated files directly)
scripts-info.ruler:dry-run: Preview what ruler:apply would regenerate without writing any files (note: skips the dprint fmt pass, so blank-line noise it reports may format away)
scripts-info.test: App unit + asset-pipeline unit (Vitest) + E2E (Playwright) — what CI runs on every push
scripts-info.perf:web: Autonomous performance profile of the web app: builds the production bundle with PERF_MARKS=true, serves it via vite preview, drives a scripted toddler session (multi-finger draw, color/size changes, erase, undo, clear) in headless Chromium under 4x CPU throttle while recording a Chrome trace + runtime metrics, then writes trace.json/metrics.json/summary.json/report.md + a screenshot to perf-profiles/. Flags: --device=phone|tablet|desktop, --throttle=N, --no-build (reuse last build)
scripts-info.perf:web:raw: perf:web without CPU throttling (raw desktop speed)
scripts-info.perf:mount: Autonomous page-load (mount) profile — the Lighthouse-TBT window scenario.mjs skips: builds the production bundle, then records a Chrome trace ACROSS the initial navigation in headless Chromium (phone viewport, 4x CPU throttle, Slow-4G network emulation), capturing load-phase long tasks via a buffered longtask observer plus paint timings. Writes trace.json (feed to perf:analyze) + mount-summary.json to perf-profiles/. Flags: --device=phone|tablet|desktop, --throttle=N, --no-throttle, --no-build
scripts-info.perf:android: Autonomous performance profile of the REAL Capacitor WebView on a connected Android emulator/device (local-only): rebuilds + installs the native app with PERF_MARKS=true, finds the WebView's DevTools socket, forwards it, connects Playwright over CDP, and runs the same toddler session + capture as perf:web (no CPU throttle — the device is the target). --no-build profiles the installed app as-is. Writes to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:ios: Autonomous performance profile in Playwright's WebKit (the same WebKit+JavaScriptCore engine the iOS WKWebView runs), driving the production preview build. WebKit has no CDP trace, so this captures the engine.* user-timing marks + FPS (not the main-thread/CPU breakdown); it profiles the engine, not the Simulator app. For device-accurate numbers, record a Safari Web Inspector Timeline against the running Simulator and feed it to perf:analyze. Writes to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:undo: Focused undo profile (ADR-0066 snapshot stack): builds the production bundle with PERF_MARKS=true, serves it via vite preview with the /dev/engine harness unlocked, and drives seven deliberately-shaped sessions (long squiggles; short dot/dash marks; a mix; five-finger drags; pen back-and-forth scribbles; crayon squiggles; crayon scribbles with mid-stroke pass splits) in headless Chromium under 4x CPU throttle. Records per scenario — via getUndoDebug() — snapshot depth, live rasters vs encoded blob bytes, plus engine.draw/commit/snapshot-copy/undo timing and history memory while resident. Writes trace.json/summary.json/report.md + undo-scenarios.json/.md to perf-profiles/. Flags: --throttle=N, --no-throttle, --no-build, --scenarios=key1,key2 (subset)
scripts-info.perf:analyze: Re-run the trace analyzer over a saved profile dir or trace.json (regenerates summary.json + report.md without re-capturing — the path a native-exported trace takes): npm run perf:analyze -- perf-profiles/<dir>
scripts-info.perf:serve: Serve the current production build on the LAN (0.0.0.0:4173) with the /dev/* harness routes unlocked (PUBLIC_ENABLE_DEV_HARNESS=true), so a real iPad/phone on the same Wi-Fi can reach http://<mac-lan-ip>:4173/ to record real input (scripts/perf/ipad-recorder.js) or /dev/engine. Build first with: PERF_MARKS=true PUBLIC_ENABLE_DEV_HARNESS=true npm run build. Stop it before perf:replay (same port). See the profiling skill's ipad-device-profiling.md
scripts-info.perf:replay: Replay a REAL finger-input recording (captured on-device with scripts/perf/ipad-recorder.js) through the engine under the profiler, so the harness measures actual human strokes instead of synthetic squiggles: opens /dev/engine, sizes the canvas to the recorded device, replays the pointer stream + UI actions at recorded timing (real frame pacing), captures a CDP trace + engine marks, and reports how your input was stored (keyframes/commands/op counts). npm run perf:replay -- --recording=<file.json> [--turbo] [--throttle=N] [--no-build]. Writes trace/report + replay-summary to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:ios:analyze: Analyze a Safari Web Inspector Timeline export from a REAL iOS device (a different format from perf:analyze — {recording:{records,markers}}, mark-only, ring-buffered, ~1ms clock). Recovers per-op main-thread cost (undo/keyframe/commit) from the enclosing timeline record, plus paint/composite and frame stats: npm run perf:ios:analyze -- <export>.json. See the profiling skill's ipad-device-profiling.md
scripts-info.test:asset-gen: Asset-gen pipeline tests (Vitest, Node env): image-analysis helpers against committed fixtures plus mocked generator CLI workflows — no Gemini/network
scripts-info.test:e2e:headed: Playwright with browser visible, slowed down (SLOWMO=500)
scripts-info.test:android: Android smoke test, self-contained: boots a headless emulator, builds + installs, runs Maestro, tears down
scripts-info.test:android:device: Maestro smoke test against an already-running emulator/device (what CI uses; same flow works on a booted iOS simulator)
scripts-info.test:ios: iOS smoke test, self-contained: boots a simulator, builds + installs, runs Maestro, tears down (macOS-only)
scripts-info.test:api:smoke: API contract smoke test, self-contained: boots a throwaway vite dev with test env, exercises the admin auth flow + a public oracle against the documented /api/* shapes, tears down (no Gemini/Blobs needed)
scripts-info.test:blobs:smoke: Deploy smoke test (ADR-0025): against a real Netlify deploy (BLOBS_SMOKE_URL + ADMIN_ACCESS_TOKEN), asserts persistent:true and round-trips a token through Blobs to prove the deployed function has the Blobs context; cleans up after itself
scripts-info.test:driver:smoke: App-driver smoke test: boots the app and drives scripts/lib/app-driver.mjs (openAppPage + expandDrawer + pickColor + setStrokeSize + drawStroke) against current markup, so the uncovered gen:* drivers can't rot silently on selector/import changes
scripts-info.redteam: MANUAL AI-safety red-team (ADR-0023): boots a throwaway vite dev, decrypts the corpus, sends each safe-*/block-* drawing to a real Gemini call, and writes an HTML input->output report to tests/redteam/output/ for human review (opens in your browser). Pass ids to run a subset (npm run redteam -- block-gun) to iterate on one drawing. Real GEMINI_API_KEY + REDTEAM_FIXTURE_KEY required; excluded from npm test by design
scripts-info.redteam:encrypt: Encrypt tests/redteam/source/*.png -> tests/redteam/encrypted/*.enc (commit the .enc); needs REDTEAM_FIXTURE_KEY
scripts-info.model-eval: MANUAL image-model bake-off: A/B-compares the two candidate production image models over web/tests/model-eval/inputs/ using the exact production request config, and writes a self-contained quality/cost/latency report to web/tests/model-eval/output/<runId>/report.html. Real GEMINI_API_KEY required; excluded from npm test. Env: SAMPLES, FILTER (id substring), CONCURRENCY, OUT_TAG, SKIP_REPORT; RESUME=<run dir> fills only the missing/failed cells of an existing run (never regenerates an image already on disk); REPORT_FROM=<run dir> (+ optional VERDICT_FILE) rebuilds report.html from an existing results.json with no API calls
scripts-info.model-eval:fixtures: Regenerate the ~45 local eval input drawings (coloring-book/night/magic-brush/scribble/freehand categories) from the real coloring assets + palette; deterministic. Env: FILTER, DEBUG_SAMPLE. Leaves gen__* (Gemini-authored) inputs intact
scripts-info.model-eval:gen-inputs: Author a few canvas-plausible input drawings with gemini-3.1-flash-image (gen__* prefix) and add them to the eval corpus; needs GEMINI_API_KEY
scripts-info.gen:icons: Generate src/lib/components/icon-names.d.ts (typed IconName union) from the SVGs in src/lib/icons/ (auto-runs before build)
scripts-info.gen:releases: Generate src/lib/releases.json + fastlane store changelogs from releases/*.md (auto-runs before build)
scripts-info.gen:large-image: Regenerate static/large-image.png by replaying large-image.svg onto the live canvas (Playwright)
scripts-info.gen:style-covers: Regenerate the AI style thumbnails in static/styles/ from source.svg via Gemini (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- --style X --temperature N for one style / different takes)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-chalk: Generate a page's CHALK outline (dedicated dark-mode line art, stored ink-on-white as .chalk.webp) from its pen outline via Gemini — solid-white eye whites/catchlights, gated on registration + new-ink checks; candidates land in .coloring-samples-dark/chalk/ (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- page-or-category… [--apply] [--max-attempts N] [-t F] [--notes "…"] [--force]; --rescore re-gates saved candidates offline)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills: Generate gated flat-color LIGHT fill candidates via Gemini into .coloring-samples/; --apply ships only when every requested page passes, otherwise committed raw/punched assets stay unchanged (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- page-or-category [--apply] [--samples N] [--temperature N])
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills:audit: Audit the committed raw fills (tools/asset-gen/fill-src/) for outline drift vs their line art (global + worst-tile keep); flags any that need regenerating. No API key/network (-- page-or-category, --overlay to dump drift maps)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills:audit:eyes: Audit the committed raw fills' EYES — flags a fill whose outlined eye rings were flooded one flat color, plus an `orb` column that scores the WHOLE pupil on the chalk-over-night composite and flags blank white orbs (chalk sclera + fill catchlight stacked over a solid-pen pupil — the band-blind class judgeNightEyes misses). Deterministic, no API key/network (-- page-or-category… to filter)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills:audit:eyes:review: Build a self-contained HTML review of every BLANK-ORB-flagged night eye (the `orb` column of gen:coloring-fills:audit:eyes) — whole-page composite + zoomed eye crops with median/white — to publish with the Artifact tool and triage real defects vs over-flags before burning them down. Deterministic, no API key/network (-- page-or-category…, --out FILE)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills:audit:shapes: Audit the committed raw fills for INVENTED COLORED SHAPES floating on the open background (extra stars/flowers no drift/white/eye gate can see; anchoring to source lines or the border is the discriminator). Deterministic, no API key/network (-- page-or-category…[.light|.night], --verbose, --overlay to dump detection maps)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills:audit:halo: Rank every shipped night fill by RESIDUAL DARK HALO around the chalk strokes after the punch (re-inked outlines/drop-shadows the lineWhite gate misses); top of the table needs human crop review. Deterministic, no API key/network (-- page-or-category… to filter, --out FILE for per-page JSON to diff across punch changes)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-outlines:audit: Audit shipped line art for SOLID black regions (they invert to white blobs in dark mode; ADR pending); lists offenders for gen:coloring-outlines:normalize. Deterministic, no API key/network (-- page-or-category… to filter)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-outlines:fresh: Author a BRAND-NEW pen outline for a page from a text scene description (no conditioning on the old drawing) via Gemini, gated on solidity/ring-depth/eye-cores/border; candidates land in .coloring-samples/fresh/ (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- page --scene "…" [--eyes] [--apply] [--max-attempts N] [-t F] [--notes "…"])
scripts-info.gen:coloring-outlines:normalize: Redraw an outline's solid black regions as thin outlined strokes via Gemini, gated on solidity + outline registration; candidates land in .coloring-samples-dark/normalize/ (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- page… [--apply] [--max-attempts N] [-t F] [--notes "…"])
scripts-info.gen:coloring-golden:freeze: Freeze every offline audit score for the whole coloring catalog (solidity/eye rings, light keep/localKeep + eyes, night drift/bgLuma/lineWhite + eyes/blank-orbs) into tools/asset-gen/golden/golden-scores.json — the committed regression baseline. Deterministic, no API key/network (~1 min)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-golden:diff: Re-run every offline audit and diff against golden/golden-scores.json: verdict flips (including composite blank-orbs) and bad-direction score movements = regressions (exit 1); improvements/moves reported. Run after any pipeline or asset change; re-freeze to adopt intended changes. No API key/network (~1 min)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-punch: Re-derive the shipped fills-only .light/.night.webp from the raw fills in tools/asset-gen/fill-src/ by punching their outlines out (line art as mask). Deterministic, no API key/network (-- page-or-category… to filter)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-thumbs: Regenerate the .thumb.webp grid thumbnails beside every coloring cover/page for the picker (-- category… to filter)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-book-proof-sheet: Build a self-contained HTML proof sheet of ONE coloring category — line art, chalk, light + night fills side by side with outline-keep scores, plus the composited view — to review/publish as an Artifact (-- category[/page[-orient]], --source shipped|samples, --out FILE)
scripts-info.gen:icons-sheet: Build the icon-gallery artifact page (ADR-0059): every icon shipped in web/src/lib/icons/ rendered at size and split into colorful spot illustrations vs monochrome UI glyphs (by chroma). Self-contained HTML; publish with artifacts:publish. Deterministic, no network (-- --out FILE; defaults to a gitignored scratch dir)
scripts-info.gen:assets:manifest: Rewrite tools/asset-gen/golden/asset-manifest.sha256 (sha256 per committed art asset: coloring pages, style covers, fill-src raws) so binary asset changes review as text diffs; verified in CI by check:assets:manifest
scripts-info.gen:shots: Regenerate the store screenshots + feature graphic for both stores into store-assets/ (Playwright)
scripts-info.artifacts:publish: Promote a keeper run output into the committed artifacts/ tree and rebuild the index — copies a file or dir under artifacts/<type>/<name> for a live GitHub Pages URL (ADR-0059). Usage: -- <source> <type>/<name>, e.g. -- lighthouse-reports lighthouse/latest
scripts-info.artifacts:index: Rebuild only artifacts/index.html (the Pages landing page) from the current artifacts/ tree — no copy
scripts-info.cap:sync: build:cap + copy web assets & plugins into the native projects
scripts-info.cap:android: cap:sync + open the Android project in Android Studio
scripts-info.cap:ios: cap:sync + open the iOS project in Xcode (macOS-only)
scripts-info.android:setup: One-time emulator setup: installs the API 33 system image and creates the Pixel_7_Pro_API_33 AVD
scripts-info.android:emulator: cap:sync + build, install, and launch on the Pixel_7_Pro_API_33 emulator
scripts-info.android:live: Run on the emulator with live reload against the dev server on port 5173
scripts-info.android:run: Build + install the debug app onto the connected device/emulator
scripts-info.android:run:device: cap:sync + build, install onto the physical 'SM-S938U1' phone (ANDROID_SERIAL=R5CY128YMGF; use when an emulator is also connected — find your serial with adb:devices)
scripts-info.android:verify: Verify the release AAB's signature (expect 'jar verified')
scripts-info.android:open: Reveal the release bundle folder in the OS file manager
scripts-info.ios:build: Debug build for the iOS Simulator → ios/App/build/Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/App.app (macOS-only)
scripts-info.ios:run: cap:sync + build, install, and launch, prompting to choose the iOS simulator or connected device (macOS-only)
scripts-info.ios:run:emulator: cap:sync + build, install, and launch on the iPad mini (A17 Pro) simulator (macOS-only; use ios:run to pick a device)
scripts-info.ios:run:device: cap:sync + build, install, and launch on the physical 'Kyle’s iPad' device (macOS-only; needs signing + a trusted developer cert — see the mobile guide)
scripts-info.ios:live: Run on a simulator/device with live reload against the dev server on port 5173 (macOS-only; pair with dev:cap)
scripts-info.ios:archive: Release archive (signed; needs the Apple Developer team set in Xcode) → ios/App/build/App.xcarchive (macOS-only)
scripts-info.ios:ipa: ios:archive + export the App Store .ipa → ios/App/build/ipa/ (macOS-only; see the mobile guide)
scripts-info.ios:open: Reveal the exported .ipa folder in the OS file manager
scripts-info.ios:clean: Xcode clean of the App scheme (macOS-only)
scripts-info.adb:reverse: Forward the phone's port 5173 to the desktop dev server (re-run after each USB reconnect)
scripts-info.update:browserslist: Refresh the browserslist database (caniuse-lite) to clear outdated-browserslist warnings
scripts-info.release: Cut a release from releases/<version>.md: bump versions, commit, tag, push (use the /release command to draft notes first)
